name: qa
description: Lightweight end-to-end QA workflow for mono- or multi-repo projects: plan and test skills that stand up a real local stack (isolated git worktrees, dynamically-free ports, dev-vs-mock data), smoke-check it, then run a per-phase scenario checklist — frontend via Playwright MCP (sequential, browser is a singleton), mobile/React Native via Maestro MCP (sequential, device is a singleton), backend via curl (parallel-safe). qa:plan interviews you one question at a time and writes a self-contained docs/qa/<target>/plan.md; qa:test executes that plan and returns a verdict (blockers, warnings, summary) without ever fixing code.
author: Gabriel Masson

interface.shortDescription: Plan and run a real end-to-end QA of a local stack, then report a verdict — never fixes code.
